page: Amazon Web Services r8idn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance belonging to the r8idn family. It is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance features 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 dedicated physical cores with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.

This instance includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering direct-attached high-speed storage. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, which offloads virtualization functions to dedicated hardware. The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps, and the instance is optimized for both network throughput and Amazon Elastic Block Store performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8idn.4xlarge provides a dense allocation of memory and local NVMe storage relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for workloads requiring high memory capacity without the overhead of a larger vCPU footprint. Recommended workloads include memory-intensive applications, relational and non-relational databases utilizing local scratch space, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring high network and EBS throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for resource-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz, it provides 16 vCPUs across 8 dedicated physical cores and 128.0 GB of system memory. The instance features 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering high-speed local instance store volumes. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the r8idn.4xlarge is optimized for both network and EBS performance, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. This hardware profile is engineered for memory-heavy applications, databases, and caching systems that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage, offering qualitative cost efficiency by balancing high memory capacity with dedicated compute resources.
